item.text: The claim is recorded with the risky absolute word `only`. I’m searching for both direct Okta references and broader SSO/SAML identity-provider references so the judgment can catch missing dependencies or alternate providers.

Direct `Okta` search returned no matches, which is already a warning sign for the draft. I’m checking lower-case and protocol-level terms next, because SSO integrations are often configured around SAML/OIDC rather than the vendor name.

item.text: The repository evidence points to Rippling as another SSO-enabling path. I’m reading the implementation and the checks now so the final judgment is tied to specific source lines rather than search previews.

arguments.summary: SSO eligibility is true when a company has a Rippling integration or Okta is enabled; login returns SSO only if eligible.
SSO eligibility is true when a company has a Rippling integration or Okta is enabled; login returns SSO only if eligible.

structured_content.summary: SSO eligibility is true when a company has a Rippling integration or Okta is enabled; login returns SSO only if eligible.

item.text: Implementation evidence contradicts the “only Okta” claim: `is_sso_eligible` accepts either Rippling integration or Okta. I’m also reading the tests/checks for corroboration.

arguments.summary: Checks cover both Rippling-based SSO and Okta-based SSO, plus password fallback when neither is set.
Checks cover both Rippling-based SSO and Okta-based SSO, plus password fallback when neither is set.

structured_content.summary: Checks cover both Rippling-based SSO and Okta-based SSO, plus password fallback when neither is set.

item.text: There is also an `sso_enabled` field on `Company`, so I’m checking whether it participates elsewhere before calling the dependency picture complete.
The extra field appears only as a dataclass field and not in the eligibility decision. I’m recording a conservative contradiction now, with the safer support-facing wording.

arguments.contradiction_notes: The implementation allows SSO when either has_rippling_integration(company) is true or company.okta_enabled is true. The checks also assert that a company with a Rippling integration gets sso-login, so Okta is not the only SSO dependency/path.
arguments.reasoning_summary: The absolute claim fails because SSO eligibility is based on Rippling integration OR Okta. Direct Okta references exist, but Rippling is a separate enabling condition for the same SSO login behavior. The sso_enabled field was searched and only appears as an unused dataclass field in app/sso.py.
arguments.safer_wording: SSO can be enabled through Okta or through a Rippling integration; it is not Okta-only.
arguments.limits: Searched local repository evidence for Okta/okta, sso, rippling, and sso_enabled. Did not inspect external services or runtime configuration outside this fixture repository.

          "contradiction_notes": "The implementation allows SSO when either has_rippling_integration(company) is true or company.okta_enabled is true. The checks also assert that a company with a Rippling integration gets sso-login, so Okta is not the only SSO dependency/path."
        }

        "judgment": {
          "claim_id": "c1",
          "status": "contradicted",
          "evidence_ids": [
            "ev-d53fe5e6719e35cc0571ca43",
            "ev-c21c2b63dbf51152b04f753e"
          ],
          "reasoning_summary": "The absolute claim fails because SSO eligibility is based on Rippling integration OR Okta. Direct Okta references exist, but Rippling is a separate enabling condition for the same SSO login behavior. The sso_enabled field was searched and only appears as an unused dataclass field in app/sso.py.",
          "limits": "Searched local repository evidence for Okta/okta, sso, rippling, and sso_enabled. Did not inspect external services or runtime configuration outside this fixture repository.",
          "safer_wording": "SSO can be enabled through Okta or through a Rippling integration; it is not Okta-only.",
          "contradiction_searched": true,
          "contradiction_notes": "The implementation allows SSO when either has_rippling_integration(company) is true or company.okta_enabled is true. The checks also assert that a company with a Rippling integration gets sso-login, so Okta is not the only SSO dependency/path."
        }
      }
    ],
    "evidence": [
      {
        "id": "ev-d53fe5e6719e35cc0571ca43",
        "claim_id": "c1",
        "claim_ids": [
          "c1"
        ],
        "file": "app/sso.py",
        "start_line": 1,
        "end_line": 22,
        "excerpt": "from dataclasses import dataclass\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass Company:\n    rippling_integration_id: str | None = None\n    okta_enabled: bool = False\n    sso_enabled: bool = False\n\n\ndef has_rippling_integration(company: Company) -> bool:\n    return bool(company.rippling_integration_id)\n\n\ndef is_sso_eligible(company: Company) -> bool:\n    return has_rippling_integration(company) or company.okta_enabled\n\n\ndef start_sso_login(company: Company) -> str:\n    if not is_sso_eligible(company):\n        return \"password-login\"\n    return \"sso-login\"",
        "excerpt_returned": true,
        "summary": "SSO eligibility is true when a company has a Rippling integration or Okta is enabled; login returns SSO only if eligible.",
        "evidence_category": "main implementation path",
        "sha256": "2f9923914e8aa95fd16ee0768375910625df3ae21f971f5521db358f1da9a923",
        "cache_hit": false,
        "cache_key": "d53fe5e6719e35cc0571ca4306a75b4b010b9308a788f05398b3068ab1886af2"
      },
      {
        "id": "ev-c21c2b63dbf51152b04f753e",
        "claim_id": "c1",
        "claim_ids": [
          "c1"
        ],
        "file": "checks/sso_checks.py",
        "start_line": 1,
        "end_line": 13,
        "excerpt": "from app.sso import Company, start_sso_login\n\n\ndef rippling_company_uses_sso():\n    assert start_sso_login(Company(rippling_integration_id=\"ri_123\")) == \"sso-login\"\n\n\ndef okta_company_uses_sso():\n    assert start_sso_login(Company(okta_enabled=True)) == \"sso-login\"\n\n\ndef default_company_uses_password_login():\n    assert start_sso_login(Company()) == \"password-login\"",
        "excerpt_returned": true,
        "summary": "Checks cover both Rippling-based SSO and Okta-based SSO, plus password fallback when neither is set.",
        "evidence_category": "tests",
        "sha256": "c3a7a2165b3d2b5b43a1b6bf0b9bb3de0795d048ec700961424004f0a0479055",
        "cache_hit": false,
        "cache_key": "c21c2b63dbf51152b04f753e66b65984e2a3460d0e0a5bf56343a1a87c61f42f"
      }

structured_content.markdown:
# Plumbref Report

Verdict: Contradicted claims found - Do not claim as written
Verification mode: explanation
Budget mode: normal
Template: Explain flow (`explain_flow` v1.0)

## Verification Outcome
- Answer gate: Do not claim as written
- Why: At least one claim is contradicted by source evidence.
- Reliance outcome: 0 safe, 0 qualified, 1 avoid
- Do not rely on:
  - contradicted: SSO only depends on Okta. Safer wording: SSO can be enabled through Okta or through a Rippling integration; it is not Okta-only.

## Answer Under Review
SSO only depends on Okta.

## Issues That Need Action

### contradicted: SSO only depends on Okta.
- Type: unknown
- Risk: medium
- Reasoning: The absolute claim fails because SSO eligibility is based on Rippling integration OR Okta. Direct Okta references exist, but Rippling is a separate enabling condition for the same SSO login behavior. The sso_enabled field was searched and only appears as an unused dataclass field in app/sso.py.
- Limits: Searched local repository evidence for Okta/okta, sso, rippling, and sso_enabled. Did not inspect external services or runtime configuration outside this fixture repository.
- Safer wording: SSO can be enabled through Okta or through a Rippling integration; it is not Okta-only.
- Contradiction pass: yes
- Evidence:
  - `app/sso.py:1-22` [main implementation path]: SSO eligibility is true when a company has a Rippling integration or Okta is enabled; login returns SSO only if eligible.

    ```text
    from dataclasses import dataclass
    
    
    @dataclass
    class Company:
        rippling_integration_id: str | None = None
        okta_enabled: bool = False
        sso_enabled: bool = False
    
    
    def has_rippling_integration(company: Company) -> bool:
        return bool(company.rippling_integration_id)
    
    
    def is_sso_eligible(company: Company) -> bool:
        return has_rippling_integration(company) or company.okta_enabled
    
    
    def start_sso_login(company: Company) -> str:
        if not is_sso_eligible(company):
            return "password-login"
        return "sso-login"
    ```
  - `checks/sso_checks.py:1-13` [tests]: Checks cover both Rippling-based SSO and Okta-based SSO, plus password fallback when neither is set.

    ```text
    from app.sso import Company, start_sso_login
    
    
    def rippling_company_uses_sso():
        assert start_sso_login(Company(rippling_integration_id="ri_123")) == "sso-login"
    
    
    def okta_company_uses_sso():
        assert start_sso_login(Company(okta_enabled=True)) == "sso-login"
    
    
    def default_company_uses_password_login():
        assert start_sso_login(Company()) == "password-login"
    ```

## Supported Claims
None recorded.

## What Wasn't Checked And Why It Matters
